---
title: "Soil_Chamber_EnviroVariable_Figs" ##time is in AK daylight time** NEE/RECO/GPP are in gC/m2/s; FCO2 and FCH4 are in gC/m2/s, flux_CO2 is in umol/m2/s; flux_CH4 is in nano mol/m2/s
output: html_document
date: "2025-04-21"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

#Load libraries 
```{r, include=FALSE}
rm(list= ls())

library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(cowplot)
library(openair)
library(plotrix)
library(signal)
library(svMisc)
library(zoo)
library(stringr)
library(plyr)
library(viridis)
library(lubridate)
library(tidyverse)
library(gridExtra)
library(plotly)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(pracma)
library(dplyr)
library(openair)
library(nlme)
library(lme4)

Sys.setenv(TZ='UTC')

```

#Load filtered and merged df of soil chamber fluxes, moisture, temp (I upload multiples but only using df_NEE_RECO2 and df_NEE_RECO2_GPP for analysis below)
```{r}
# #filtered for p<0.05; units umol/m2/s or nmol/m2/s
# df_soilchambers_filtered = fread('C:/Users/kkent/Documents/Council Data/Soil Chambers_Council/council_filtered_soil_chamber_fluxes_2017to2019.csv')
# 
# #fluxes and moisture/temp df merged; FCO2 in units g/m2/s
# df_fulljoin = fread('C:/Users/kkent/Documents/Council Data/Soil Chambers_Council/council_fulljoin_soilchamber_fluxes_moisttemp_2017to2019.csv')

# *****************Use these two, above are just extra if needed for looking at*********************

#used transparent and opaque chambers to identify NEE and RECO, then merged back together 
df_NEE_RECO2 = fread('C:/Users/kkent/Documents/Council Data/Soil Chambers_Council/council_fulljoin_soilchamber_fluxes_moisttemp_2017to2019.csv')

#calculated GPP (NEE - Reco)
df_NEE_RECO2_GPP = fread('C:/Users/kkent/Documents/Council Data/Soil Chambers_Council/council_NEE_RECO2_GPP_2017to2019.csv')



```


#Re-shape df into long format 
```{r}
library(tidyr)

#Remove the NAs 
library(dplyr)

# Reshape the dataframe to long format, choose variables of interest 
df_wide <- df_NEE_RECO2_GPP %>%
  select(plot_ID, plot_type, landscape_position, measurement_date, time, date, VWC, air_temp, flux_CO2, flux_CH4, FCH4, NEE, RECO, GPP, inundated, soil_temp_10_cm, thawdepth) %>%
  #remove NAs
   filter(complete.cases( VWC, air_temp, flux_CO2, flux_CH4, FCH4, NEE, RECO, GPP, inundated, soil_temp_10_cm, thawdepth)) #exclude soil temp 15 cm since most of it is NA 

#make NEE, GPP, RECO, and FCH4 all g/m2/s -> umol/m2/s
df_wide$NEE_umol <- df_wide$NEE * 1/12 * 1000000
df_wide$GPP_umol <- df_wide$GPP * 1/12 * 1000000
df_wide$RECO_umol <- df_wide$RECO * 1/12 * 1000000
df_wide$FCH4_umol <- df_wide$FCH4 * 1/12 * 1000000




df_long2 <- df_NEE_RECO2_GPP %>%
  select(plot_ID, plot_type, landscape_position, measurement_date, time, date, VWC, air_temp, flux_CO2, flux_CH4, FCH4, NEE, RECO, GPP, inundated, soil_temp_10_cm, thawdepth) %>%
  #remove NAs
   filter(complete.cases( VWC, air_temp, flux_CO2, flux_CH4, FCH4, NEE, RECO, GPP, inundated, soil_temp_10_cm, thawdepth)) %>% #exclude soil temp 15 cm since most of it is NA 
  pivot_longer(cols = c(NEE, RECO, GPP, FCH4),
               names_to = "flux_type",
               values_to = "flux_value")

#df_long2$FCH4_umol <- df_long2$FCH4 * 1/12 * 1000000
df_long2$flux_umol <- df_long2$flux_value * 1/12 * 1000000

colnames(df_wide)
colnames(df_long2)
```


#looking at variables  across landscape positions & inundation
```{r}

ggplot(df_long2, aes(x = landscape_position, y = flux_umol, color = flux_type)) +
  geom_boxplot(position = "dodge") +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", position = position_dodge(0.8), shape = 23, size = 4)+
    facet_wrap(~inundated) +
  labs(title = "Observed NEE by Land pos",
       x = "Landscape Position",
       y = "NEE (flux_value)") +
  theme_bw()

ggplot(df_long2, aes(x = landscape_position, y = flux_umol, fill = flux_type)) +
  geom_boxplot(position = position_dodge(0.8)) +
  stat_summary(
    fun = mean,
    geom = "point",
    shape = 23,
    size = 4,
    position = position_dodge(0.8)  # same dodge width as boxplot
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Observed NEE by Landscape Position",
    x = "Landscape Position",
    y = "Flux Value (umol/m2.s)",
    fill = "Flux Type"
  ) +
  theme_bw()



#thawdepth by landpos
thaw_landpos <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = landscape_position, y = thawdepth)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Observed Thaw Depth by Landscape Position",
       x = "Landscape Position",
       y = "Thaw Depth (cm)") +
  theme_bw() +
  stat_summary(fun=mean, geom="point", shape=23, size=4)

thaw_landpos

#thawdepth by landpos and inundated 
thaw_landposinun <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = landscape_position, y = thawdepth)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
   facet_wrap(~inundated) +
  labs(title = "Observed thawdepth by Landscape Position & Inundation",
       x = "Landscape Position",
       y = "Thaw Depth (cm)") +
  theme_bw() +
  stat_summary(fun=mean, geom="point", shape=23, size=4)

thaw_landposinun

#VWC by landpos 
VWC_landpos <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = landscape_position, y = VWC)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
    labs(title = "Observed VWC by Landscape Position",
       x = "Landscape Position",
       y = "VWC (%)") +
  theme_bw() +
  stat_summary(fun=mean, geom="point", shape=23, size=4)

VWC_landpos

#VWC by landpos and inundated
VWC_landposinun <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = landscape_position, y = VWC)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~inundated)+
    labs(title = "Observed VWC by Landscape Position & Inundation",
       x = "Landscape Position",
       y = "VWC (%)") +
  theme_bw() +
  stat_summary(fun=mean, geom="point", shape=23, size=4)

VWC_landposinun


#soil temp by landpos 
soiltemp_landpos <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = landscape_position, y = soil_temp_10_cm)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
    labs(title = "Observed Soil Temp by Landscape Position",
       x = "Landscape Position",
       y = "Soil Temp (C)") +
  theme_bw() +
  stat_summary(fun=mean, geom="point", shape=23, size=4)

soiltemp_landpos


#soiltemp by landpos and inundated 
soiltemp_landposinun <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = landscape_position, y = soil_temp_10_cm)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
    facet_wrap(~inundated)+
    labs(title = "Observed Soil Temp by Landscape Position & Inundation",
       x = "Landscape Position",
       y = "Soil Temp (C)") +
  theme_bw() +
  stat_summary(fun=mean, geom="point", shape=23, size=4)

soiltemp_landposinun

#air temp by landpos 
airtemp_landpos <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = landscape_position, y = air_temp)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
    labs(title = "Observed Air Temp by Landscape Position",
       x = "Landscape Position",
       y = "Air Temp (C)") +
  theme_bw() +
  stat_summary(fun=mean, geom="point", shape=23, size=4)

airtemp_landpos

#airtemp by landpos and inundated 
airtemp_landposinun <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = landscape_position, y = air_temp)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
    facet_wrap(~inundated)+
    labs(title = "Observed Air Temp by Landscape Position & Inundation",
       x = "Landscape Position",
       y = "Air Temp (C)") +
  theme_bw() +
  stat_summary(fun=mean, geom="point", shape=23, size=4)

airtemp_landposinun

#Number of inundated plots by landpos 
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

df_counts <- df_long2 %>%
  distinct(plot_ID, landscape_position, inundated) %>%  # keep unique plots only
  count(landscape_position, inundated)                  # count per group

ggplot(df_counts, aes(x = landscape_position, y = n, fill = inundated)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  labs(
    title = "Unique Plot Counts by Landscape Position and Inundation",
    x = "Landscape Position",
    y = "Count of Unique Plots",
    fill = "Inundated"
  ) +
  theme_bw() 
  


n_distinct(df_long$plot_ID) #35 total plots

```
#Arrange timeseries figures into a grid to save as single figure
```{r}
# Install if you don't have it
 install.packages("patchwork")
library(patchwork)

# Arrange plots in two columns (3 rows x 2 columns) 
combined_plot <- (title1 + title2) /
                 (soiltemp_landpos + soiltemp_landposinun) /
                 (airtemp_landpos + airtemp_landposinun) /
                 (thaw_landpos + thaw_landposinun)/
                 (VWC_landpos + VWC_landposinun) 
                # plot_layout(guides = "collect") # Collects all legends into one
combined_plot



#remove titles from each individual plot 
library(patchwork)

combined_plot <- (
  
  soiltemp_landpos + labs(title = NULL) + 
  soiltemp_landposinun + labs(title = NULL)
) /
(
  airtemp_landpos + labs(title = NULL) + 
  airtemp_landposinun + labs(title = NULL)
) /
(
  thaw_landpos + labs(title = NULL) + 
  thaw_landposinun + labs(title = NULL)
) /
(
  VWC_landpos + labs(title = NULL) + 
  VWC_landposinun + labs(title = NULL)
) 


combined_plot






```

#save image to maintain dimensions 
```{r}
ggsave(
  filename = "C:/Users/kkent/Documents/Github Flux Network/Council_Flux_Analysis_Paper/Council Figures/Soil Chambers/enviro_variables_landposinun.png",  # File name and extension
  plot = combined_plot,        # The plot to save (default is the last plot created)
  width = 20,                 # Width in inches
  height = 20,                # Height in inches
  dpi = 600,                 # Resolution in dots per inch
  units = "cm"               # Units for width and height (can be "in", "cm", or "mm")
)
```

#Adding column titles to the combined fig 
```{r}
library(patchwork)
library(ggplot2)

# First, remove individual titles from each plot
soiltemp_landpos <- soiltemp_landpos + theme(plot.title = element_blank())
soiltemp_landposinun <- soiltemp_landposinun + theme(plot.title = element_blank())
airtemp_landpos <- airtemp_landpos + theme(plot.title = element_blank())
airtemp_landposinun <- airtemp_landposinun + theme(plot.title = element_blank())
thaw_landpos <- thaw_landpos + theme(plot.title = element_blank())
thaw_landposinun <- thaw_landposinun + theme(plot.title = element_blank())
VWC_landpos <- VWC_landpos + theme(plot.title = element_blank())
VWC_landposinun <- VWC_landposinun + theme(plot.title = element_blank())

# Create empty plots for column titles
title1 <- ggplot() + 
  annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 0.5, label = "Landscape Position", size = 5, fontface = "bold") + 
  theme_void()

title2 <- ggplot() + 
  annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 0.5, label = "Landscape Position and Inundation", size = 5, fontface = "bold") + 
  theme_void()

# Arrange plots with column titles
combined_plot2 <- (title1 + title2) /
                 (soiltemp_landpos + soiltemp_landposinun) /
                 (airtemp_landpos + airtemp_landposinun) /
                 (thaw_landpos + thaw_landposinun) /
                 (VWC_landpos + VWC_landposinun) +
                 plot_layout(heights = c(0.5, 1, 1, 1, 1)) #, widths = c(0.3, 3)) #widths not working, may adjust later 


# # Add overall bottom title if needed
# combined_plot2 <- combined_plot + 
#   plot_annotation(
#     caption = "Landscape Position",
#     theme = theme(plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12, face = "bold"))
#   )

combined_plot2
```
#save image to maintain dimensions 
```{r}
ggsave(
  filename = "C:/Users/kkent/Documents/Github Flux Network/Council_Flux_Analysis_Paper/Council Figures/Soil Chambers/enviro_variables_landposinun2.png",  # File name and extension
  plot = combined_plot2,        # The plot to save (default is the last plot created)
  width = 20,                 # Width in inches
  height = 20,                # Height in inches
  dpi = 600,                 # Resolution in dots per inch
  units = "cm"               # Units for width and height (can be "in", "cm", or "mm")
)
```

#Testing differences in enviro features among landscapes - exclude inundated slope**




#Correlation matrix 

####Create correlation matrix dataset of variables of interest - can only use continuous variables* 

```{r}
# Environmental relationships
# Create a correlation plot between fluxes and environmental variables

#can only use numeric variables, so landscape_position and inundated can't be used 
chamber_corr_matrix <- df_long %>%
  select(NEE, GPP, RECO, thawdepth, soil_temp_10_cm, VWC, air_temp) %>%
  rename(
    Soil_Temp_10cm = soil_temp_10_cm,
    Soil_VWC = VWC,
    Air_Temperature = air_temp,
    Thaw_Depth = thawdepth
  )

#make sure you have complete cases, if not already 
#cor_matrix2 <- cor(chamber_corr_matrix, use = "complete.obs")
```


####Pearson Corr - Flux v Environmental correlation matrix - soil chambers 
*But this is for normally distributed data, which most of these fluxes/data are not, so see Spearman corr in code chunk below - keeping both for reference 
```{r}
# Calculate the correlation matrix
chamber_corr_matrix_result <- cor(chamber_corr_matrix , use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
print(chamber_corr_matrix_result)

# Convert correlation matrix to long format for plotting
cor_long2 <- reshape2::melt(chamber_corr_matrix_result)
names(cor_long2) <- c("Var1", "Var2", "Correlation")



ggplot(cor_long2, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "red", mid = "white", high = "blue", midpoint = 0,
                        limits = c(-1, 1)) +  # Add this to set the full range to be 1 to -1
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
       plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)) +                 
  labs(title = "Correlation Matrix of Soil Chamber GPP Fluxes and Environmental Variables")
   



# option 2: Fewer breaks for a cleaner look, only labels corr values above 0.2 --> I like this one best 
#this uses linear correlation / pearson correlation *** beware if your data is not normally distributed 
ggplot(cor_long2, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_tile() +
  # Add correlation values only for strong correlations
  geom_text(data = subset(cor_long2, abs(Correlation) > 0.2),  # Only show stronger correlations
            aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", Correlation),
                 color = abs(Correlation) < 0.5), # Changes text color based on correlation strength
            size = 3,
            fontface = "bold") + #to make numbers in tiles bold
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "red", 
                      mid = "white", 
                      high = "blue", 
                      midpoint = 0,
                      limits = c(-1, 1),
                      breaks = c(-1, -0.5, 0, 0.5, 1)) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("white", "black"), guide = "none") +  # For text visibility
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)) +                 
  labs(title = "Correlation Matrix of Soil Chamber C Fluxes and Environmental Variables")

 #saves the figure directly above
ggsave("C:/Users/kkent/Documents/Github Flux Network/Council_Flux_Analysis_Paper/Council Figures/Soil Chambers/soil_chamber_Cflux_corr_matrix.png",
       width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 600)

```


#Correlation matrix - spearman for non-parametric 

####Spearman Corr
```{r}
#calc correlation matrix with spearman corr
chamber_SPcorr_result <- cor(chamber_corr_matrix, method = "spearman", use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
print(chamber_SPcorr_result)

#For p-values, use rcorr
library(Hmisc)
chamber_corr_with_p <- rcorr(as.matrix(chamber_corr_matrix), type = "spearman")
print(chamber_corr_with_p)

# View results
print(chamber_SPcorr_result)
print("P-values:")
print(chamber_corr_with_p$P)

#correlation coeffs
#                   NEE   GPP  RECO Thaw_Depth Soil_Temp_10cm Soil_VWC Air_Temperature
# NEE              1.00 -0.75  0.13       0.16           0.09    -0.11            0.18
# GPP             -0.75  1.00  0.48      -0.13           0.20     0.11            0.17
# RECO             0.13  0.48  1.00      -0.02           0.51     0.09            0.60
# Thaw_Depth       0.16 -0.13 -0.02       1.00           0.18     0.34           -0.06
# Soil_Temp_10cm   0.09  0.20  0.51       0.18           1.00     0.19            0.64
# Soil_VWC        -0.11  0.11  0.09       0.34           0.19     1.00            0.11
# Air_Temperature  0.18  0.17  0.60      -0.06           0.64     0.11            1.00
# 
# n= 155 
# 
# 
# P values 
#                 NEE    GPP    RECO   Thaw_Depth Soil_Temp_10cm Soil_VWC Air_Temperature
# NEE                    0.0000 0.0967 0.0463     0.2819         0.1796   0.0257         
# GPP             0.0000        0.0000 0.0947     0.0111         0.1713   0.0380         
# RECO            0.0967 0.0000        0.8328     0.0000         0.2771   0.0000         
# Thaw_Depth      0.0463 0.0947 0.8328            0.0235         0.0000   0.4957         
# Soil_Temp_10cm  0.2819 0.0111 0.0000 0.0235                    0.0199   0.0000         
# Soil_VWC        0.1796 0.1713 0.2771 0.0000     0.0199                  0.1590         
# Air_Temperature 0.0257 0.0380 0.0000 0.4957     0.0000         0.1590   
```

#Plot correlation matrix - tiled figure 
```{r}
# Convert correlation matrix to long format for plotting
cor_long2 <- reshape2::melt(chamber_SPcorr_result )
names(cor_long2) <- c("Var1", "Var2", "Correlation")


#correlation matrix using spearman's corr, for non-parametric / not normally distributed data 
ggplot(cor_long2, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_tile() +
  # Add correlation values only for strong correlations
  geom_text(data = subset(cor_long2, abs(Correlation) > 0.3),  # Only show stronger correlations
            aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", Correlation),
                 color = abs(Correlation) < 0.5), # Changes text color based on correlation strength
            size = 3,
            fontface = "bold") + #to make numbers in tiles bold
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "red", 
                      mid = "white", 
                      high = "blue", 
                      midpoint = 0,
                      limits = c(-1, 1),
                      breaks = c(-1, -0.5, 0, 0.5, 1)) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("white", "black"), guide = "none") +  # For text visibility
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)) +                 
  labs(title = "Correlation Matrix of Soil Chamber C Fluxes and Environmental Variables")


```





```{r}
#double checking 
cor.test(df_long$NEE, df_long$GPP, method="spearman")
cor.test(chamber_corr_matrix$NEE, chamber_corr_matrix$GPP, method="spearman")
cor.test(chamber_corr_matrix$RECO, chamber_corr_matrix$GPP, method="spearman")
```

#Adding significance codes to correlation matrix figure - coding help from Claude, keeping the redundant steps for reference 
```{r}
# Step 1: Calculate correlation and p-values
library(Hmisc)
corr_result <- rcorr(as.matrix(chamber_corr_matrix), type = "spearman")

# Step 2: Extract correlation coefficients and p-values
cor_coef <- corr_result$r
cor_p <- corr_result$P

# Step 3: Create a long format data frame for plotting
cor_long <- reshape2::melt(cor_coef)
names(cor_long) <- c("Var1", "Var2", "Correlation")

# Step 4: Add p-values to the long format data
p_long <- reshape2::melt(cor_p)
cor_long$p_value <- p_long$value

# Step 5: Add significance indicators
cor_long$significance <- ""
cor_long$significance[cor_long$p_value < 0.05] <- "*"    # p < 0.05
cor_long$significance[cor_long$p_value < 0.01] <- "**"   # p < 0.01
cor_long$significance[cor_long$p_value < 0.001] <- "***" # p < 0.001

# Step 6: Create the correlation plot with significance indicators
ggplot(cor_long, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_tile() +
  # Add correlation coefficients with significance stars
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(Correlation, 2), significance)), 
            color = "black", size = 3) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "red", mid = "white", high = "blue", midpoint = 0,
                      limits = c(-1, 1)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)) +
  labs(title = "Correlation Matrix of Soil Chamber C Fluxes and Environmental Variables")




#correlation matrix using spearman's corr, for non-parametric / not normally distributed data 
ggplot(cor_long2, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_tile() +
  # Add correlation values only for strong correlations
  geom_text(data = subset(cor_long, abs(Correlation) > 0.3),  # Only show stronger correlations
            aes(label = paste0(sprintf("%.2f", Correlation), significance),
                 color = abs(Correlation) < 0.5), # Changes text color based on correlation strength
            size = 4.5,
            fontface = "bold") + #to make numbers in tiles bold
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "red", 
                      mid = "white", 
                      high = "blue", 
                      midpoint = 0,
                      limits = c(-1, 1),
                      breaks = c(-1, -0.5, 0, 0.5, 1)) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("white", "black"), guide = "none") +  # For text visibility
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)) +                 
  labs(title = "Correlation Matrix of Soil Chamber C Fluxes and Environmental Variables")


#Display corr text on tiles if it's >0.3 OR if it has a sig p 

ggplot(cor_long2, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_tile() +
  # Add correlation values for EITHER strong correlations OR significant ones
  geom_text(data = subset(cor_long2, abs(Correlation) > 0.3 | p_value < 0.05), #the | represents "OR" so this displays correlations above 0.3 OR significant correlations; also use "abs" to use absolute value of the corr so it's printed whether it's positive or negative*  
            aes(label = paste0(sprintf("%.2f", Correlation), significance),
                color = abs(Correlation) < 0.5), # Changes text color based on correlation strength
            size = 4.5,
            fontface = "bold") + #to make numbers in tiles bold
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "red", 
                      mid = "white", 
                      high = "blue", 
                      midpoint = 0,
                      limits = c(-1, 1),
                      breaks = c(-1, -0.5, 0, 0.5, 1)) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("white", "black"), guide = "none") +  # For text visibility
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)) +                 
  labs(title = "Correlation Matrix of Soil Chamber C Fluxes and Environmental Variables")

 #saves the figure directly above
ggsave("C:/Users/kkent/Documents/Github Flux Network/Council_Flux_Analysis_Paper/Council Figures/Soil Chambers/soil_chamber_Cflux_corr_matrix.png",
       width = 9, height = 5, dpi = 600)
```

```{r}
ggplot(df_long, aes(x=thawdepth, y = NEE))+
  geom_point()
```
# ** TO DO************
```{r}
# Create visualization functions to reuse
plot_flux_by_position <- function(flux_var, df_long) {
  ggplot(df_long, aes_string(x = "landscape_position", y = flux_var, fill = "landscape_position")) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    facet_wrap(~inundated, labeller = labeller(inundated = c("N" = "Not Inundated", "Y" = "Inundated"))) +
    labs(title = paste(flux_var, "by Landscape Position and Inundation Status"),
         x = "Landscape Position", y = flux_var) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
}

# Create scatter plots with smooth lines for continuous variables
plot_flux_by_continuous <- function(flux_var, cont_var, df_long) {
  ggplot(df_long, aes_string(x = cont_var, y = flux_var, color = "inundated")) + #color = landpos 
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
    geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE) +
    facet_wrap(~landscape_position) +
    labs(title = paste(flux_var, "vs", cont_var, "by Landscape Position"),
         x = cont_var, y = flux_var) +
    theme_minimal()
}

# Generate these plots for each flux
plot_flux_by_position("GPP_umol", df_long)
plot_flux_by_position("NEE_umol", df_long)
plot_flux_by_position("RECO_umol", df_long)
plot_flux_by_position("GPP_umol", df_long)
plot_flux_by_position("FCH4_umol", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous("GPP_umol", "soil_temp_10_cm", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous("GPP_umol", "air_temp", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous("GPP_umol", "VWC", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous("GPP_umol", "thawdepth", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous("NEE_umol", "soil_temp_10_cm", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous("NEE_umol", "air_temp", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous("NEE_umol", "VWC", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous("NEE_umol", "thawdepth", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous("RECO_umol", "soil_temp_10_cm", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous("RECO_umol", "air_temp", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous("RECO_umol", "VWC", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous("RECO_umol", "thawdepth", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous("FCH4_umol", "soil_temp_10_cm", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous("FCH4_umol", "air_temp", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous("FCH4_umol", "VWC", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous("FCH4_umol", "thawdepth", df_long)
```

```{r}
# Create scatter plots with smooth lines for continuous variables, faceted by both landscape position and inundation # loess = smooth line, lm = linear line
plot_flux_by_position2 <- function(flux_var, df_long) {
  ggplot(df_long, aes_string(x = "landscape_position", y = flux_var, fill = "landscape_position")) +
    geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~inundated, 
               labeller = labeller(inundated = c("N" = "Not Inundated", "Y" = "Inundated"))) +
    labs(title = paste(flux_var, "by Landscape Position and Inundation Status"),
         x = "Landscape Position", y = flux_var) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
}


plot_flux_by_continuous2 <- function(flux_var, cont_var, df_long) {
  ggplot(df_long, aes_string(x = cont_var, y = flux_var, color = "inundated")) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) + #method = "loess"
    facet_wrap(~landscape_position) +
    labs(title = paste(flux_var, "vs", cont_var, "by Landscape Position and Inundation Status"),
         x = cont_var, y = flux_var) +
    theme_minimal()
}

plot_flux_by_position2("GPP_umol", df_long)
plot_flux_by_position2("NEE_umol", df_long)
plot_flux_by_position2("RECO_umol", df_long)
plot_flux_by_position2("GPP_umol", df_long)
plot_flux_by_position2("FCH4_umol", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous2("GPP_umol", "soil_temp_10_cm", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous2("GPP_umol", "air_temp", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous2("GPP_umol", "VWC", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous2("GPP_umol", "thawdepth", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous2("NEE_umol", "soil_temp_10_cm", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous2("NEE_umol", "air_temp", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous2("NEE_umol", "VWC", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous2("NEE_umol", "thawdepth", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous2("RECO_umol", "soil_temp_10_cm", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous2("RECO_umol", "air_temp", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous2("RECO_umol", "VWC", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous2("RECO_umol", "thawdepth", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous2("FCH4_umol", "soil_temp_10_cm", df_long)
plot_flux_by_continuous2("FCH4_umol", "air_temp", df_long)

plot_flux_by_continuous2("FCH4_umol", "thawdepth", df_long)
```

#Looking at fluxes with interacting enviro variables 
```{r}
# NEE and soil + VWC
ggplot(df_long, aes(x = soil_temp_10_cm, y = NEE_umol, color = VWC)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "NEE Flux vs Soil Temp",
       subtitle = "Colored by Soil Water Content",
       x = "Temperature",
       y = "NEE Flux")


#This code breaks SWC into 3 even levels (low, intermediate, high) to show the change in temp relationship depending on SWC
ggplot(df_long, aes(x = soil_temp_10_cm, y = NEE_umol)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = VWC), alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(aes(group = cut(VWC, breaks = 3)), method = "lm") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "NEE Flux vs Temperature",
       subtitle = "Colored by Soil Water Content",
       x = "Temperature (°C)",
       y = "NEE Flux",
       color = "Soil Water Content")


```
#Bins - for soil temp, air temp, VWC, and thawdepth  
```{r}
#check ranges of variables 
summary(df_long$VWC) #min = 0, max = 90.3
summary(df_long$soil_temp_10_cm) #min = 0.6, max = 16.5
summary(df_long$thawdepth) #min= 10, max = 118cm
summary(df_long$air_temp) #min = 3.7, max = 27.10 

# Create VWC bins
df_long$VWC_bin <- cut(df_long$VWC, 
                       breaks = c(-0.1, 1, 20, 40, 60, 95), #max VWC is 90.30
                       labels = c("Dry (0-1%)",
                                "Very Dry (1-20%)",
                                "Dry (20-40%)",
                                "Wet (40-60%)",
                                "Very Wet (60-95%)"))
                          #include.lowest = TRUE)



# Create soil temperature bins - min is 6, max is 16.4 
df_long$soiltemp_bin <- cut(df_long$soil_temp_10_cm, 
                       breaks = c(0, 4, 8, 12, 16.5),  
                       labels = c("Cold (0 to 4°C)",
                                "Cool (4 to 8°C)",
                                "Warm (8 to 12°C)",
                                "Warmest (12 to 16.5°C)"))

# Create soil temperature bins - min is 6, max is 16.4 
df_long$thawdepth_bin <- cut(df_long$thawdepth, 
                       breaks = c(0, 25, 50, 75, 120),  
                       labels = c("Shallowest (0 to 25cm)",
                                "Shallow (25 to 50cm)",
                                "Deep (50 to 75cm)",
                                "Deepest (75 to 120cm)"))


# Create soil temperature bins - min is 6, max is 16.4 
df_long$airtemp_bin <- cut(df_long$air_temp, 
                       breaks = c(0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 30),  
                       labels = c("Cold (0 to 5°C)",
                                "Cool (5 to 10°C)",
                                "Warm (10 to 15°C)",
                                "Warmer (15 to 20°C)",
                                "Hot (20 - 30°C"))
```

#to test whether a linear or quadratic or polynomial line fits data best 
```{r}
# For a specific bin
bin_data <- df_long[df_long$VWC_bin == "Wet (40-60%)",]

# Fit linear model
linear_model <- lm(NEE_umol ~ soil_temp_10_cm, data = bin_data)

# Fit quadratic model
quadratic_model <- lm(NEE_umol ~ soil_temp_10_cm + I(soil_temp_10_cm^2), data = bin_data)

# Compare models
anova(linear_model, quadratic_model)

# Get R-squared and p-values for the better model
summary(quadratic_model)
```


#Interaction of Enviro Vars
```{r}
# soiltemp and VWC 
ggplot(df_long, aes(x = soil_temp_10_cm, y = VWC, color = VWC)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  facet_wrap(~VWC_bin, scales = "free") +
  scale_color_viridis_c() +
  labs(title = "VWC vs Temperature by Moisture Range",
       x = "Soil Temperature (°C)",
       y = "VWC",
       color = "VWC (%)") +
  theme_minimal()

#NEE x soiltemp and thawdepth
ggplot(df_long, aes(x = soil_temp_10_cm, y = NEE_umol, color = thawdepth)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  facet_wrap(~thawdepth_bin, scales = "free") +

       x = "Soil Temperature (°C)",
       y = "NEE Flux",
       color = "Thaw Depth (cm)") +
  theme_minimal()


#NEE x airtemp and VWC
ggplot(df_long, aes(x = air_temp, y = NEE_umol, color = VWC)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  facet_wrap(~VWC_bin, scales = "free") +
  scale_color_viridis_c() +
  labs(title = "NEE vs Air Temperature by VWC Range",
       x = "AirTemperature (°C)",
       y = "NEE Flux",
       color = "VWC (%)") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
```
