```{r}
  scale_color_viridis_c() +
  labs(title = "NEE vs Air Temperature by VWC Range",
       x = "AirTemperature (°C)",
       y = "NEE Flux",
       color = "VWC (%)") +
  theme_minimal()




```




```{r}
# Function to test differences by landscape position
test_by_position <- function(flux_var, df_long) {
  # Formula creation
  formula <- as.formula(paste(flux_var, "~ landscape_position"))
  
  # Check normality first
  shapiro_test <- shapiro.test(df_long[[flux_var]])
  
  if (shapiro_test$p.value < 0.05) {
    # Non-parametric test if not normal
    kruskal_result <- kruskal.test(formula, data = df_long)
    return(list(test = "Kruskal-Wallis", result = kruskal_result))
  } else {
    # ANOVA if normal
    anova_result <- aov(formula, data = df_long)
    return(list(test = "ANOVA", result = summary(anova_result), 
                posthoc = TukeyHSD(anova_result)))
  }
}

# Test interaction with inundation
test_interaction <- function(flux_var, df_long) {
  formula <- as.formula(paste(flux_var, "~ landscape_position * inundated"))
  model <- aov(formula, data = df_long)
  return(summary(model))
}

# Apply to each flux
 gpp_position_test <- test_by_position("GPP", df)
 gpp_interaction <- test_interaction("GPP", df)
```


#RLM and KW tests of continuous vars by landscape position 
```{r}
rlm1 <- rlm(VWC ~ landscape_position, data = df_NEE)
Anova(rlm1, type = "II") 

#soil temp x landpos p = 0.7, not sig 
#VWC p < 0.001 *SIG* 
#air temp p=0.8, not sig 
#thawdepth p < 0.001 *SIG*

rlm2 <- rlm(air_temp ~ landscape_position, data = df_NEE)
Anova(rlm2, type = "II") 

#soil temp x landpos p = 0.7, not sig 
#VWC p < 0.001 *SIG* 
#air temp p=0.8, not sig 
#thawdepth p < 0.001 *SIG*

# Test landscape position on continuous variable 
kruskal.test(thawdepth ~ landscape_position, data = df_NEE)

#soil temp x landpos p = 0.6, not sig 
#VWC p < 0.001 *SIG* 
#air temp p=0.8, not sig 
#thawdepth p < 0.001 *SIG*

# Test inundation on continuous variable 
kruskal.test(air_temp ~ inundated, data = df_NEE)

#soil temp x inundated p = 0.008 *SIG 
#VWC p < 0.001 *SIG* 
#air temp p=0.4, not sig 
#thawdepth p < 0.001 *SIG*

#suggests VWC and thawdepth vary significantly among landpos; and all variables except for air temp vary significantly by inundation --> maybe run PCA on this?


```
#Variance partitioning - coding help from Claude 
```{r}
# Venn diagram showing how much variance in your environmental variables (thaw depth, soil temperature, and VWC) is explained by landscape position and inundation. Shows the unique variance explained by each factor, plus their shared variance.


library(vegan)
# Prepare data
env_vars <- df_long %>% select(thawdepth, soil_temp_10_cm, VWC)
cat_vars <- df_long %>% select(landscape_position, inundated)

# Convert categorical variables to dummy variables
cat_dummy <- model.matrix(~ landscape_position + inundated - 1, data = cat_vars)

# Variance partitioning
vp <- varpart(env_vars, 
              cat_dummy[,grep("landscape", colnames(cat_dummy))], 
              cat_dummy[,grep("inundated", colnames(cat_dummy))])

# Plot results
plot(vp, digits = 2, Xnames = c("Landscape Position", "Inundation"))

#Shows landpos along explains ~3% of variance in the numeric enviro factors, inundation explains about 11% of variance, and their shared variance = ~ 5%. Residuals show 80% of variance in the enviro factors is explained by something else 



library(vegan)
# Prepare data
env_vars2 <- df_long %>% select(thawdepth, soil_temp_10_cm, VWC)
flux_varFCH4 <- df_long %>% select(FCH4)
flux_varNEE <- df_long %>% select(NEE)
flux_vars <- df_long %>% select(NEE, GPP, RECO)
cat_vars2 <- df_long %>% select(landscape_position, inundated)

# Convert categorical variables to dummy variables
cat_dummy <- model.matrix(~ landscape_position + inundated - 1, data = cat_vars2)

# Variance partitioning
vp2 <- varpart(flux_vars, 
              cat_dummy[,grep("landscape", colnames(cat_dummy))], 
              cat_dummy[,grep("inundated", colnames(cat_dummy))])

# Plot results
plot(vp2, digits = 2, Xnames = c("Landscape Position", "Inundation"))
```




#PCA - only works with continuous/numeric data 

```{r}
# Modified PCA code
library(FactoMineR)

env_vars <- select(df_long, soil_temp_10_cm, air_temp, VWC, thawdepth)
pca_result <- PCA(env_vars, graph = FALSE)

# Extract the coordinates and add back the categorical variables
pca_df <- data.frame(pca_result$ind$coord)
pca_df$landscape_position <- df_long$landscape_position
pca_df$inundated <- df_long$inundated

# Now create the plot
ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = Dim.1, y = Dim.2, color = landscape_position, shape = inundated)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  stat_ellipse() +
  labs(title = "PCA of Environmental Variables")
```


#PCA for each landscape position 
```{r}
# Function to create PCA for a specific landscape position
create_position_pca <- function(position_name, data) {
  # Filter data for the specific landscape position
  position_data <- data[data$landscape_position == position_name, ]
  
  # Select environmental variables
  env_vars <- position_data[, c("soil_temp_10_cm", "air_temp", "VWC", "thawdepth")]
  
  # Run PCA if we have enough data
  if(nrow(env_vars) > 3) { # Need at least 4 rows for PCA with 4 variables
    pca_result <- prcomp(env_vars, scale. = TRUE)
    
    # Create plot data
    pca_plot_data <- data.frame(
      PC1 = pca_result$x[,1],
      PC2 = pca_result$x[,2],
      inundated = position_data$inundated
    )
    
    # Create the plot
    plot <- ggplot(pca_plot_data, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, shape = inundated, color = inundated)) +
      geom_point(size = 3) +
      stat_ellipse() +
      labs(title = paste("PCA of Environmental Variables -", position_name),
           x = "PC1", y = "PC2") +
      theme_minimal()
    
    # Additional info to return
    var_explained <- summary(pca_result)$importance[2, 1:2] * 100
    loadings <- pca_result$rotation
    
    return(list(plot = plot, 
                variance_explained = var_explained, 
                loadings = loadings,
                pca_result = pca_result))
  } else {
    return(NULL)
  }
}

# Apply to each landscape position
lowland_pca <- create_position_pca("lowland", df_long)
slope_pca <- create_position_pca("slope", df_long)
upland_pca <- create_position_pca("upland", df_long)

# View the plots
lowland_pca$plot
slope_pca$plot
upland_pca$plot

# Check which environmental variables drive variation within each position
lowland_pca$loadings
slope_pca$loadings
upland_pca$loadings

#LOWLAND
#                       PC1        PC2        PC3        PC4
# soil_temp_10_cm 0.6024493  0.2616177 -0.4371061 -0.6144504
# air_temp        0.4259289  0.6731998  0.1778673  0.5777108
# VWC             0.5030634 -0.3491144  0.7649874 -0.1996011
# thawdepth       0.4500741 -0.5970572 -0.4382873  0.4988589

#SLOPE
#                        PC1        PC2        PC3        PC4
# soil_temp_10_cm -0.6003161  0.2636395  0.6747952  0.3387716
# air_temp        -0.3520804  0.6710585 -0.2793839 -0.5896308
# VWC             -0.6086657 -0.2416271 -0.6448954  0.3940207
# thawdepth       -0.3810328 -0.6494544  0.2251798 -0.6183179

#UPLAND
#                        PC1        PC2        PC3         PC4
# soil_temp_10_cm -0.6500843 -0.3233936  0.1376162 -0.67369777
# air_temp        -0.6969717 -0.1066950 -0.1408980  0.69497793
# VWC              0.2081485 -0.6482817 -0.7313549 -0.03905338
# thawdepth        0.2197611 -0.6810019  0.6529392  0.24821732

# To add carbon fluxes as supplementary variables
position_data <- df_long[df_long$landscape_position == "lowland", ]
env_vars <- position_data[, c("soil_temp_10_cm", "air_temp", "VWC", "thawdepth")]
flux_vars <- position_data[, c("GPP", "NEE", "RECO", "FCH4")]

pca_result <- PCA(env_vars, graph = FALSE, 
                  quanti.sup = 1:ncol(flux_vars), # Add flux variables as supplementary
                  row.w = rep(1, nrow(env_vars)))
```


#PCA of continuous variables and C fluxes 
```{r}
# Step 1: Run PCA on your environmental variables
env_vars <- df_long %>% 
  select(soil_temp_10_cm, air_temp, VWC, thawdepth)

# Run PCA
pca_result <- prcomp(env_vars, scale = TRUE)

# Summary of PCA
summary(pca_result)

# Look at variable contributions to principal components
pca_loadings <- pca_result$rotation
print(pca_loadings)

# Step 2: Plot the PCA biplot
biplot(pca_result, scale = 0)

# Step 3: Enhanced visualization with ggplot2
library(ggplot2)
library(ggfortify)

# Create PCA biplot with ggplot2

#NEE
autoplot(pca_result, data = df_long, colour = 'NEE_umol', shape = 'inundated',
         loadings = TRUE, loadings.colour = 'blue',
         loadings.label = TRUE, loadings.label.size = 4) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "PCA of Environmental Variables",
       subtitle = "Colored by NEE flux value")

#Gpp
autoplot(pca_result, data = df_long, colour = 'GPP_umol', shape = 'inundated',
         loadings = TRUE, loadings.colour = 'blue',
         loadings.label = TRUE, loadings.label.size = 4) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "PCA of Environmental Variables",
       subtitle = "Colored by GPP flux value")
#RECO
autoplot(pca_result, data = df_long, colour = 'RECO_umol', shape = 'inundated',
         loadings = TRUE, loadings.colour = 'blue',
         loadings.label = TRUE, loadings.label.size = 4) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "PCA of Environmental Variables",
       subtitle = "Colored by RECO flux value")

#FCH4
autoplot(pca_result, data = df_long, colour = 'FCH4_umol', shape = 'inundated',
         loadings = TRUE, loadings.colour = 'blue',
         loadings.label = TRUE, loadings.label.size = 4) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "PCA of Environmental Variables",
       subtitle = "Colored by FCH4 flux value")



# Step 4: Correlation between PC scores and carbon fluxes
df_with_pcs <- cbind(df_long, pca_result$x)

# Correlation between principal components and carbon fluxes
cor_pcs_fluxes <- cor(df_with_pcs[, c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3", "PC4")], 
                      df_with_pcs[, c("NEE_umol", "GPP_umol", "RECO_umol", "FCH4_umol")],
                      method = "spearman")
print(cor_pcs_fluxes)

#suggests PC1 is a thermal gradient (mainly contributed to by air temp and soil temp), and PC2 is a moisture & thaw gradient, driven by VWC and thawdepth
#PC1 - pos values = cooler temps (due to negative relationship in loadings, so inverse relationship), neg values = warmer 
#PC2 - pos values = wetter soils and deeper thaw depths, neg values = drier soils and shallow thaw depths 
#Pc1 = ~42% of variance, so thermal plays a large role 
#PC2 = ~35% of variance
```

#Piece-wise SEM

#### using glm 
```{r}
library(piecewiseSEM)

#dataset without any NAs
df_long_cc <- df_long%>%
  select(plot_ID, plot_type, landscape_position, measurement_date, time, date, VWC, air_temp, FCH4_umol, NEE_umol, RECO_umol, GPP_umol, inundated, soil_temp_10_cm, thawdepth)


any(is.na(df_long_cc))

# Create dummy variables for landscape position since it can only handle numeric data 
df_long_cc$lp_slope <- ifelse(df_long_cc$landscape_position == "slope", 1, 0)
df_long_cc$lp_lowland <- ifelse(df_long_cc$landscape_position == "lowland", 1, 0)
#df_long_cc$lp_upland <- ifelse(df_long_cc$landscape_position == "upland", 1, 0) #swapped out for lowland to get lowland values, using upland as reference point
df_long_cc$inundated_numeric <- ifelse(df_long_cc$inundated == "Y", 1, 0)


# Create individual models - make sure these are all lm or glm objects
model_soil <- glm(soil_temp_10_cm ~ lp_slope + lp_lowland + inundated_numeric, data = df_long_cc)
model_air <- glm(air_temp ~ lp_slope + lp_lowland + inundated_numeric, data = df_long_cc)
model_vwc <- glm(VWC ~ lp_slope + lp_lowland + inundated_numeric, data = df_long_cc)
model_thaw <- glm(thawdepth ~ lp_slope + lp_lowland + inundated_numeric, data = df_long_cc)
model_nee <- glm(NEE_umol ~ soil_temp_10_cm + air_temp + VWC + thawdepth + lp_slope + lp_lowland + inundated_numeric, data = df_long_cc)

# Specify the model structure correctly
psem_model <- psem(
  model_soil,
  model_air,
  model_vwc, 
  model_thaw,
  model_nee
)

# Get summary
summary(psem_model)

plot(psem_model)
```

#Visualizing piecewise SEM - pSEM

#TO DO: include lowland landpos 

```{r}
#from glm pSEM - pSEM does not work with rlm 

#have to add coefficients in by hand ** based on pSEM results 

library(DiagrammeR)

# Create a basic path diagram
grViz("
  digraph path_model {
    rankdir=LR;
    
    # Nodes
    node [shape=box]
    lp_slope [label='Slope Position']
    lp_upland [label='Upland Position']
    inundated [label='Inundation']
    
    node [shape=ellipse]
    soil_temp [label='Soil Temp']
    air_temp [label='Air Temp']  
    vwc [label='VWC']
    thaw [label='Thaw Depth']
    nee [label='NEE']
    
    # Edges with significant paths
    lp_slope -> vwc [label='-0.18*']
    lp_upland -> vwc [label='-0.24**'] 
    lp_upland -> thaw [label='-0.22*']
    inundated -> soil_temp [label='0.20*']
    inundated -> vwc [label='0.39***']
    inundated -> thaw [label='0.31***']
    
    soil_temp -> air_temp [style=dashed, label='']
    air_temp -> nee [label='0.33**']
    vwc -> nee [label='-0.19*']
    vwc -> thaw [style=dashed, label=''] 
    air_temp -> thaw [style=dashed, label='']
    thaw -> nee [label='0.16.']
  }
")
```
#TO DO
#Need to add lowland in....? maybe run psem with diff reference point to get the lowland effect **
```{r}
library(DiagrammeR)

# Create a complete path diagram that includes lowland position
grViz("
  digraph path_model {
    rankdir=LR;
    
    # Nodes
    node [shape=box]
    lp_lowland [label='Lowland Position']
    lp_slope [label='Slope Position']
    lp_upland [label='Upland Position']
    inundated [label='Inundation']
    
    node [shape=ellipse]
    soil_temp [label='Soil Temp']
    air_temp [label='Air Temp']  
    vwc [label='VWC']
    thaw [label='Thaw Depth']
    nee [label='NEE']
    
    # Edges for lowland (as reference category)
    lp_lowland -> vwc [label='+', color='green']
    lp_lowland -> thaw [label='+', color='green']
    
    # Edges for slope and upland (using actual coefficients)
    lp_slope -> vwc [label='-0.18*', color='red']
    lp_upland -> vwc [label='-0.24**', color='red'] 
    lp_upland -> thaw [label='-0.22*', color='red']
    
    # Other significant paths
    inundated -> soil_temp [label='0.20*', color='green']
    inundated -> vwc [label='0.39***', color='green']
    inundated -> thaw [label='0.31***', color='green']
    
    # Relationships between environmental variables  
    soil_temp -> air_temp [style=dashed]
    air_temp -> nee [label='0.33**', color='green']
    vwc -> nee [label='-0.19*', color='red']
    vwc -> thaw [style=dashed] 
    air_temp -> thaw [style=dashed]
    thaw -> nee [label='0.16', color='green']
    
    # Visual enhancements
    {rank=same; lp_lowland; lp_slope; lp_upland; inundated}
    {rank=same; soil_temp; air_temp; vwc; thaw}
  }
")
```


```{r}
# For each component model in your piecewise SEM
# Extract and check the residuals

# Access your individual models in the psem directly
model_list <- list(
  soil_temp = model_soil,
  air_temp = model_air,
  vwc = model_vwc,
  thaw = model_thaw,
  nee = model_nee
)

# Then check residuals as shown above

# Check residuals for each model
par(mfrow=c(2,2))
for (i in seq_along(model_list)) {
  model_name <- names(model_list)[i]
  if (is.null(model_name)) model_name <- paste("Model", i)
  
  resids <- residuals(model_list[[i]])
  
  # Histogram of residuals
  hist(resids, main=paste("Residuals -", model_name), 
       xlab="Residuals", freq=FALSE)
  curve(dnorm(x, mean=mean(resids), sd=sd(resids)), add=TRUE, col="red")
  
  # QQ plot
  qqnorm(resids, main=paste("QQ Plot -", model_name))
  qqline(resids, col="red")
}



# Shapiro-Wilk test for normality on each model's residuals
residual_tests <- list()
for (i in seq_along(model_list)) {
  model_name <- ifelse(is.null(names(model_list)[i]), paste("Model", i), names(model_list)[i])
  resids <- residuals(model_list[[i]])
  sw_test <- shapiro.test(resids)
  residual_tests[[model_name]] <- list(
    W = sw_test$statistic,
    p_value = sw_test$p.value,
    normal = sw_test$p.value > 0.05
  )
}

# Print results
print(residual_tests)





```


#checking linearity among flux and enviro variables 
####NEE
```{r}
# Check each relationship for linearity
# Example for NEE model
par(mfrow=c(2,2))

# Soil temp vs NEE
plot(df_long_cc$soil_temp_10_cm, df_long_cc$NEE_umol, 
     main="NEE vs Soil Temp", xlab="Soil Temp", ylab="NEE")
lines(lowess(df_long_cc$soil_temp_10_cm, df_long_cc$NEE_umol), col="red")
abline(lm(NEE_umol ~ soil_temp_10_cm, data=df_long_cc), col="blue")

# Air temp vs NEE
plot(df_long_cc$air_temp, df_long_cc$NEE_umol, 
     main="NEE vs Air Temp", xlab="Air Temp", ylab="NEE")
lines(lowess(df_long_cc$air_temp, df_long_cc$NEE_umol), col="red")
abline(lm(NEE_umol ~ air_temp, data=df_long_cc), col="blue")

# VWC vs NEE
plot(df_long_cc$VWC, df_long_cc$NEE_umol, 
     main="NEE vs VWC", xlab="VWC", ylab="NEE")
lines(lowess(df_long_cc$VWC, df_long_cc$NEE_umol), col="red")
abline(lm(NEE_umol ~ VWC, data=df_long_cc), col="blue")

# Thaw depth vs NEE
plot(df_long_cc$thawdepth, df_long_cc$NEE_umol, 
     main="NEE vs Thaw Depth", xlab="Thaw Depth", ylab="NEE")
lines(lowess(df_long_cc$thawdepth, df_long_cc$NEE_umol), col="red")
abline(lm(NEE_umol ~ thawdepth, data=df_long_cc), col="blue")
```
```{r}
# Check linearity using correlation tests
response_vars <- c("soil_temp_10_cm", "air_temp", "VWC", "thawdepth", "NEE_umol")
predictor_vars <- c("soil_temp_10_cm", "air_temp", "VWC", "thawdepth", 
                   "lp_slope", "lp_upland", "inundated_numeric")

# Compare linear correlation (pearson) vs rank correlation (non-parametric, spearman)
# If they're similar, relationship is likely linear
for (resp in response_vars) {
  cat("\nChecking linearity for response:", resp, "\n")
  
  for (pred in predictor_vars) {
    if (resp != pred) {
      pearson_r <- cor(df_long_cc[[resp]], df_long_cc[[pred]], method="pearson", use="complete.obs")
      spearman_r <- cor(df_long_cc[[resp]], df_long_cc[[pred]], method="spearman", use="complete.obs")
      
      cat(pred, "- Pearson r:", round(pearson_r, 3), 
          "Spearman r:", round(spearman_r, 3),
          "Diff:", round(abs(pearson_r - spearman_r), 3), "\n")
      
      # Large differences suggest non-linearity
      if (abs(pearson_r - spearman_r) > 0.2) {
        cat("  Potential non-linear relationship detected\n")
      }
    }
  }
}

#Potential non-linear relationships - pearson vs spearman correlations with a difference > 0.1:
```
#bootstrapping and piecewise SEM - code from Claude 

#### NEE using rlm
```{r}
library(boot)
library(piecewiseSEM)
library(MASS)

# Simplified bootstrap function focusing just on direct and indirect effects
boot_psem_robust <- function(data, indices) {
  # Create bootstrap sample
  boot_data <- data[indices, ]
  
  # Fit models on bootstrap sample - using robust regression here 
  b_soil <- rlm(soil_temp_10_cm ~ lp_slope + lp_upland + inundated_numeric, data = boot_data)
  b_air <- rlm(air_temp ~ lp_slope + lp_upland + inundated_numeric, data = boot_data)
  b_vwc <- rlm(VWC ~ lp_slope + lp_upland + inundated_numeric, data = boot_data)
  b_thaw <- rlm(thawdepth ~ lp_slope + lp_upland + inundated_numeric, data = boot_data)
  b_nee <- rlm(NEE_umol ~ soil_temp_10_cm + air_temp + VWC + thawdepth + 
             lp_slope + lp_upland + inundated_numeric, data = boot_data)
  
  # Direct effects
  direct_slope <- coef(b_nee)["lp_slope"]
  direct_upland <- coef(b_nee)["lp_upland"]
  direct_inundated <- coef(b_nee)["inundated_numeric"]
  
  # Environmental effects on NEE
  effect_soil <- coef(b_nee)["soil_temp_10_cm"]
  effect_air <- coef(b_nee)["air_temp"]
  effect_vwc <- coef(b_nee)["VWC"]
  effect_thaw <- coef(b_nee)["thawdepth"]
  
  # Landscape effects on environment
  slope_soil <- coef(b_soil)["lp_slope"]
  slope_air <- coef(b_air)["lp_slope"]
  slope_vwc <- coef(b_vwc)["lp_slope"]
  slope_thaw <- coef(b_thaw)["lp_slope"]
  
  upland_soil <- coef(b_soil)["lp_upland"]
  upland_air <- coef(b_air)["lp_upland"]
  upland_vwc <- coef(b_vwc)["lp_upland"]
  upland_thaw <- coef(b_thaw)["lp_upland"]
  
  inundated_soil <- coef(b_soil)["inundated_numeric"]
  inundated_air <- coef(b_air)["inundated_numeric"]
  inundated_vwc <- coef(b_vwc)["inundated_numeric"]
  inundated_thaw <- coef(b_thaw)["inundated_numeric"]
  
  # Individual indirect paths
  slope_via_soil <- slope_soil * effect_soil
  slope_via_air <- slope_air * effect_air
  slope_via_vwc <- slope_vwc * effect_vwc
  slope_via_thaw <- slope_thaw * effect_thaw
  
  upland_via_soil <- upland_soil * effect_soil
  upland_via_air <- upland_air * effect_air
  upland_via_vwc <- upland_vwc * effect_vwc
  upland_via_thaw <- upland_thaw * effect_thaw
  
  inundated_via_soil <- inundated_soil * effect_soil
  inundated_via_air <- inundated_air * effect_air
  inundated_via_vwc <- inundated_vwc * effect_vwc
  inundated_via_thaw <- inundated_thaw * effect_thaw
  
  # Total indirect effects
  slope_indirect <- slope_via_soil + slope_via_air + slope_via_vwc + slope_via_thaw
  upland_indirect <- upland_via_soil + upland_via_air + upland_via_vwc + upland_via_thaw
  inundated_indirect <- inundated_via_soil + inundated_via_air + inundated_via_vwc + inundated_via_thaw
  
  # Total effects
  slope_total <- direct_slope + slope_indirect
  upland_total <- direct_upland + upland_indirect
  inundated_total <- direct_inundated + inundated_indirect
  
  # Return all values as a named vector
  return(c(
    # Direct effects on NEE
    direct_slope = direct_slope,
    direct_upland = direct_upland,
    direct_inundated = direct_inundated,
    effect_soil = effect_soil,
    effect_air = effect_air,
    effect_vwc = effect_vwc,
    effect_thaw = effect_thaw,
    
    # Indirect paths
    slope_via_soil = slope_via_soil,
    slope_via_air = slope_via_air,
    slope_via_vwc = slope_via_vwc,
    slope_via_thaw = slope_via_thaw,
    upland_via_soil = upland_via_soil,
    upland_via_air = upland_via_air,
    upland_via_vwc = upland_via_vwc,
    upland_via_thaw = upland_via_thaw,
    inundated_via_soil = inundated_via_soil,
    inundated_via_air = inundated_via_air,
    inundated_via_vwc = inundated_via_vwc,
    inundated_via_thaw = inundated_via_thaw,
    
    # Total indirect effects
    slope_indirect = slope_indirect,
    upland_indirect = upland_indirect,
    inundated_indirect = inundated_indirect,
    
    # Total effects
    slope_total = slope_total,
    upland_total = upland_total,
    inundated_total = inundated_total
  ))
}

# Run bootstrap with this simplified function
boot_results_robust <- boot(data = df_long_cc, 
                    statistic = boot_psem_robust, 
                    R = 1000)

# Create summary with robust error handling
effect_summary_robust <- data.frame(
  Path = names(boot_results_robust$t0),
  Estimate = boot_results_robust$t0,
  CI_Lower = NA_real_,
  CI_Upper = NA_real_,
  Significant = NA,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Calculate CIs with robust error handling
for (i in 1:nrow(effect_summary_robust)) {
  # Check for NAs in bootstrap samples
  na_count <- sum(is.na(boot_results_robust$t[,i]))
  if (na_count > 0) {
    cat("Parameter", effect_summary_robust$Path[i], 
        "has", na_count, "missing values out of", nrow(boot_results_robust$t), "\n")
    
    # Skip if too many NAs
    if (na_count > 0.5 * nrow(boot_results_robust$t)) {
      cat("Skipping CI calculation due to excessive missing values\n")
      next
    }
  }
  
  # Try to calculate CI
  tryCatch({
    ci <- boot.ci(boot_results_robust, type = "perc", index = i)
    if (!is.null(ci) && !is.null(ci$percent)) {
      effect_summary_robust$CI_Lower[i] <- ci$percent[4]
      effect_summary_robust$CI_Upper[i] <- ci$percent[5]
      effect_summary_robust$Significant[i] <- (ci$percent[4] * ci$percent[5] > 0)
    }
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("Error calculating CI for parameter", effect_summary_robust$Path[i], ":", e$message, "\n")
  })
}

# Print summary of valid results
valid_results <- effect_summary_robust[!is.na(effect_summary_robust$Significant), ]
valid_results$Estimate <- round(valid_results$Estimate, 4)
valid_results$CI_Lower <- round(valid_results$CI_Lower, 4)
valid_results$CI_Upper <- round(valid_results$CI_Upper, 4)

# Sort by effect type
direct_effects <- valid_results[grep("^direct|^effect", valid_results$Path), ]
indirect_paths <- valid_results[grep("via", valid_results$Path), ]
indirect_totals <- valid_results[grep("indirect$", valid_results$Path), ]
total_effects <- valid_results[grep("total$", valid_results$Path), ]

# Print results
cat("\n--- DIRECT EFFECTS ---\n")
print(direct_effects[order(-abs(direct_effects$Estimate)), ])

cat("\n--- INDIRECT PATHWAYS ---\n")
print(indirect_paths[order(-abs(indirect_paths$Estimate)), ])

cat("\n--- TOTAL INDIRECT EFFECTS ---\n")
print(indirect_totals)

cat("\n--- TOTAL EFFECTS ---\n")
print(total_effects)


```
####adding in interaction effects
```{r}
# Modified bootstrap function with interaction effects
boot_psem_with_interactions <- function(data, indices) {
  # Create bootstrap sample
  boot_data <- data[indices, ]
  
  # Environmental models (can include interactions here too if needed)
  b_soil <- rlm(soil_temp_10_cm ~ lp_slope + lp_upland + inundated_numeric, data = boot_data)
  b_air <- rlm(air_temp ~ lp_slope + lp_upland + inundated_numeric, data = boot_data)
  b_vwc <- rlm(VWC ~ lp_slope + lp_upland + inundated_numeric, data = boot_data)
  b_thaw <- rlm(thawdepth ~ lp_slope + lp_upland + inundated_numeric, data = boot_data)
  
  # NEE model with key interactions
  b_nee <- rlm(NEE_umol ~ soil_temp_10_cm + air_temp + VWC + thawdepth + 
              lp_slope + lp_upland + inundated_numeric +
              # Add important interaction terms
              VWC:lp_slope + VWC:lp_upland +     # Effect of VWC varies by landscape position
              thawdepth:inundated_numeric +     # Effect of thaw depth varies by inundation
               thawdepth:air_temp +
                thawdepth:VWC +
                air_temp:soil_temp_10_cm,       
              
              data = boot_data)
  
  # Extract direct effects (now includes interaction terms)
  direct_effects <- c(
    #environmental effects on NEE 
    "effect_soil" = coef(b_nee)["soil_temp_10_cm"],
    "effect_air" = coef(b_nee)["air_temp"],
    "effect vwc" = coef(b_nee)["VWC"],        # Main effect (when lp_slope=0, lp_upland=0)
    "effect_thaw" = coef(b_nee)["thawdepth"],        # Main effect (when inundated_numeric=0)
    #landscape effects on NEE
    "direct_slope" = coef(b_nee)["lp_slope"],
    "direct_upland" = coef(b_nee)["lp_upland"],
    "direct_inundated" = coef(b_nee)["inundated_numeric"],
    # Interaction effects on NEE
    "vwc_x_slope" = coef(b_nee)["VWC:lp_slope"],
    "vwc_x_upland" = coef(b_nee)["VWC:lp_upland"],
    "thaw_x_inundated" = coef(b_nee)["thawdepth:inundated_numeric"],
    "thaw_x_VWC" = coef(b_nee)["thawdepth:VWC"],
    "thaw_x_airtemp" = coef(b_nee)["thawdepth:air_temp"],
    "airtemp_x_soiltemp" = coef(b_nee)["air_temp:soil_temp_10_cm"]
                                
  )
  
  # Landscape effects on environment
  slope_soil <- coef(b_soil)["lp_slope"]
  slope_air <- coef(b_air)["lp_slope"]
  slope_vwc <- coef(b_vwc)["lp_slope"]
  slope_thaw <- coef(b_thaw)["lp_slope"]
  
  upland_soil <- coef(b_soil)["lp_upland"]
  upland_air <- coef(b_air)["lp_upland"]
  upland_vwc <- coef(b_vwc)["lp_upland"]
  upland_thaw <- coef(b_thaw)["lp_upland"]
  
  inundated_soil <- coef(b_soil)["inundated_numeric"]
  inundated_air <- coef(b_air)["inundated_numeric"]
  inundated_vwc <- coef(b_vwc)["inundated_numeric"]
  inundated_thaw <- coef(b_thaw)["inundated_numeric"]
  
  # Individual indirect paths
  slope_via_soil <- slope_soil * effect_soil
  slope_via_air <- slope_air * effect_air
  slope_via_vwc <- slope_vwc * effect_vwc
  slope_via_thaw <- slope_thaw * effect_thaw
  
  upland_via_soil <- upland_soil * effect_soil
  upland_via_air <- upland_air * effect_air
  upland_via_vwc <- upland_vwc * effect_vwc
  upland_via_thaw <- upland_thaw * effect_thaw
  
  inundated_via_soil <- inundated_soil * effect_soil
  inundated_via_air <- inundated_air * effect_air
  inundated_via_vwc <- inundated_vwc * effect_vwc
  inundated_via_thaw <- inundated_thaw * effect_thaw
  
  # Total indirect effects
  slope_indirect <- slope_via_soil + slope_via_air + slope_via_vwc + slope_via_thaw
  upland_indirect <- upland_via_soil + upland_via_air + upland_via_vwc + upland_via_thaw
  inundated_indirect <- inundated_via_soil + inundated_via_air + inundated_via_vwc + inundated_via_thaw
  
  # Total effects
  slope_total <- direct_slope + slope_indirect
  upland_total <- direct_upland + upland_indirect
  inundated_total <- direct_inundated + inundated_indirect
  
  # Return all values as a named vector
  return(c( direct_effects,
    # Direct effects on NEE
    direct_slope = direct_slope,
    direct_upland = direct_upland,
    direct_inundated = direct_inundated,
    effect_soil = effect_soil,
    effect_air = effect_air,
    effect_vwc = effect_vwc,
    effect_thaw = effect_thaw,
    
    # Indirect paths
    slope_via_soil = slope_via_soil,
    slope_via_air = slope_via_air,
    slope_via_vwc = slope_via_vwc,
    slope_via_thaw = slope_via_thaw,
    upland_via_soil = upland_via_soil,
    upland_via_air = upland_via_air,
    upland_via_vwc = upland_via_vwc,
    upland_via_thaw = upland_via_thaw,
    inundated_via_soil = inundated_via_soil,
    inundated_via_air = inundated_via_air,
    inundated_via_vwc = inundated_via_vwc,
    inundated_via_thaw = inundated_via_thaw,
    
    # Total indirect effects
    slope_indirect = slope_indirect,
    upland_indirect = upland_indirect,
    inundated_indirect = inundated_indirect,
    
    # Total effects
    slope_total = slope_total,
    upland_total = upland_total,
    inundated_total = inundated_total
  ))
}

# Run bootstrap with this simplified function
boot_results_interact.robust <- boot(data = df_long_cc, 
                    statistic = boot_psem_with_interactions, 
                    R = 1000)

# Create summary with robust error handling
effect_summary_robust2 <- data.frame(
  Path = names(boot_results_interact.robust$t0),
  Estimate = boot_results_interact.robust$t0,
  CI_Lower = NA_real_,
  CI_Upper = NA_real_,
  Significant = NA,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Calculate CIs with robust error handling
for (i in 1:nrow(effect_summary_robust2)) {
  # Check for NAs in bootstrap samples
  na_count <- sum(is.na(boot_results_interact.robust$t[,i]))
  if (na_count > 0) {
    cat("Parameter", effect_summary_robust2$Path[i], 
        "has", na_count, "missing values out of", nrow(boot_results_interact.robust$t), "\n")
    
    # Skip if too many NAs
    if (na_count > 0.5 * nrow(boot_results_interact.robust$t)) {
      cat("Skipping CI calculation due to excessive missing values\n")
      next
    }
  }
  
  # Try to calculate CI
  tryCatch({
    ci <- boot.ci(boot_results_interact.robust, type = "perc", index = i)
    if (!is.null(ci) && !is.null(ci$percent)) {
      effect_summary_robust2$CI_Lower[i] <- ci$percent[4]
      effect_summary_robust2$CI_Upper[i] <- ci$percent[5]
      effect_summary_robust2$Significant[i] <- (ci$percent[4] * ci$percent[5] > 0)
    }
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("Error calculating CI for parameter", effect_summary_robust2$Path[i], ":", e$message, "\n")
  })
}

# Print summary of valid results
valid_results2 <- effect_summary_robust2[!is.na(effect_summary_robust2$Significant), ]
valid_results2$Estimate <- round(valid_results2$Estimate, 4)
valid_results2$CI_Lower <- round(valid_results2$CI_Lower, 4)
valid_results2$CI_Upper <- round(valid_results2$CI_Upper, 4)

# Sort by effect type
direct_effects <- valid_results2[grep("^direct|^effect", valid_results2$Path), ]
indirect_paths <- valid_results2[grep("via", valid_results2$Path), ]
indirect_totals <- valid_results2[grep("indirect$", valid_results2$Path), ]
total_effects <- valid_results2[grep("total$", valid_results2$Path), ]

# Print results
cat("\n--- DIRECT EFFECTS ---\n")
print(direct_effects[order(-abs(direct_effects$Estimate)), ])

cat("\n--- INDIRECT PATHWAYS ---\n")
print(indirect_paths[order(-abs(indirect_paths$Estimate)), ])

cat("\n--- TOTAL INDIRECT EFFECTS ---\n")
print(indirect_totals)

cat("\n--- TOTAL EFFECTS ---\n")
print(total_effects)
  

```
```{r}
# Modified bootstrap function with interaction effects
boot_psem_with_interactions <- function(data, indices) {
  # Create bootstrap sample
  boot_data <- data[indices, ]
  
  # Environmental models
  b_soil <- rlm(soil_temp_10_cm ~ lp_slope + lp_upland + inundated_numeric, data = boot_data)
  b_air <- rlm(air_temp ~ lp_slope + lp_upland + inundated_numeric, data = boot_data)
  b_vwc <- rlm(VWC ~ lp_slope + lp_upland + inundated_numeric, data = boot_data)
  b_thaw <- rlm(thawdepth ~ lp_slope + lp_upland + inundated_numeric, data = boot_data)
  
  # NEE model with key interactions
  b_nee <- rlm(NEE_umol ~ soil_temp_10_cm + air_temp + VWC + thawdepth + 
              lp_slope + lp_upland + inundated_numeric +
              # Add important interaction terms
              VWC:lp_slope + VWC:lp_upland +     
              thawdepth:inundated_numeric +
              thawdepth:air_temp +
              thawdepth:VWC +
              air_temp:soil_temp_10_cm,
              data = boot_data)
  
  # Extract environmental effects on NEE
  env_effect_soil <- coef(b_nee)["soil_temp_10_cm"]
  env_effect_air <- coef(b_nee)["air_temp"]
  env_effect_vwc <- coef(b_nee)["VWC"]
  env_effect_thaw <- coef(b_nee)["thawdepth"]
  
  # Direct landscape effects on NEE
  direct_slope <- coef(b_nee)["lp_slope"]
  direct_upland <- coef(b_nee)["lp_upland"]
  direct_inundated <- coef(b_nee)["inundated_numeric"]
  
  # Extract interaction effects  
  int_vwc_slope <- coef(b_nee)["VWC:lp_slope"]
  int_vwc_upland <- coef(b_nee)["VWC:lp_upland"]
  int_thaw_inundated <- coef(b_nee)["thawdepth:inundated_numeric"]
  int_thaw_vwc <- coef(b_nee)["thawdepth:VWC"]
  int_thaw_airtemp <- coef(b_nee)["thawdepth:air_temp"]
  int_airtemp_soiltemp <- coef(b_nee)["air_temp:soil_temp_10_cm"]
  
  # Combine all direct effects
  direct_effects <- c(
    "effect_soil" = env_effect_soil,
    "effect_air" = env_effect_air,
    "effect_vwc" = env_effect_vwc,
    "effect_thaw" = env_effect_thaw,
    "direct_slope" = direct_slope,
    "direct_upland" = direct_upland, 
    "direct_inundated" = direct_inundated,
    "int_vwc_slope" = int_vwc_slope,
    "int_vwc_upland" = int_vwc_upland,
    "int_thaw_inundated" = int_thaw_inundated,
    "int_thaw_vwc" = int_thaw_vwc,
    "int_thaw_airtemp" = int_thaw_airtemp,
    "int_airtemp_soiltemp" = int_airtemp_soiltemp
  )
  
  # Landscape effects on environment
  slope_soil <- coef(b_soil)["lp_slope"]
  slope_air <- coef(b_air)["lp_slope"]
  slope_vwc <- coef(b_vwc)["lp_slope"]
  slope_thaw <- coef(b_thaw)["lp_slope"]
  
  upland_soil <- coef(b_soil)["lp_upland"]
  upland_air <- coef(b_air)["lp_upland"]
  upland_vwc <- coef(b_vwc)["lp_upland"]
  upland_thaw <- coef(b_thaw)["lp_upland"]
  
  inundated_soil <- coef(b_soil)["inundated_numeric"]
  inundated_air <- coef(b_air)["inundated_numeric"]
  inundated_vwc <- coef(b_vwc)["inundated_numeric"]
  inundated_thaw <- coef(b_thaw)["inundated_numeric"]
  
  # Individual indirect paths - now using the correctly named variables
  slope_via_soil <- slope_soil * env_effect_soil
  slope_via_air <- slope_air * env_effect_air
  slope_via_vwc <- slope_vwc * env_effect_vwc
  slope_via_thaw <- slope_thaw * env_effect_thaw
  
  upland_via_soil <- upland_soil * env_effect_soil
  upland_via_air <- upland_air * env_effect_air
  upland_via_vwc <- upland_vwc * env_effect_vwc
  upland_via_thaw <- upland_thaw * env_effect_thaw
  
  inundated_via_soil <- inundated_soil * env_effect_soil
  inundated_via_air <- inundated_air * env_effect_air
  inundated_via_vwc <- inundated_vwc * env_effect_vwc
  inundated_via_thaw <- inundated_thaw * env_effect_thaw
  
  # Total indirect effects
  slope_indirect <- slope_via_soil + slope_via_air + slope_via_vwc + slope_via_thaw
  upland_indirect <- upland_via_soil + upland_via_air + upland_via_vwc + upland_via_thaw
  inundated_indirect <- inundated_via_soil + inundated_via_air + inundated_via_vwc + inundated_via_thaw
  
  # Total effects
  slope_total <- direct_slope + slope_indirect
  upland_total <- direct_upland + upland_indirect
  inundated_total <- direct_inundated + inundated_indirect
  
  # Create a vector of all effects to return
  all_effects <- c(
    # Direct and interaction effects
    direct_effects,
    
    # Landscape-to-environment effects
    "slope_soil" = slope_soil,
    "slope_air" = slope_air,
    "slope_vwc" = slope_vwc,
    "slope_thaw" = slope_thaw,
    "upland_soil" = upland_soil,
    "upland_air" = upland_air,
    "upland_vwc" = upland_vwc,
    "upland_thaw" = upland_thaw,
    "inundated_soil" = inundated_soil,
    "inundated_air" = inundated_air,
    "inundated_vwc" = inundated_vwc,
    "inundated_thaw" = inundated_thaw,
    
    # Indirect pathways
    "slope_via_soil" = slope_via_soil,
    "slope_via_air" = slope_via_air,
    "slope_via_vwc" = slope_via_vwc,
    "slope_via_thaw" = slope_via_thaw,
    "upland_via_soil" = upland_via_soil,
    "upland_via_air" = upland_via_air,
    "upland_via_vwc" = upland_via_vwc,
    "upland_via_thaw" = upland_via_thaw,
    "inundated_via_soil" = inundated_via_soil,
    "inundated_via_air" = inundated_via_air,
    "inundated_via_vwc" = inundated_via_vwc,
    "inundated_via_thaw" = inundated_via_thaw,
    
    # Total indirect effects
    "slope_indirect" = slope_indirect,
    "upland_indirect" = upland_indirect,
    "inundated_indirect" = inundated_indirect,
    
    # Total effects
    "slope_total" = slope_total,
    "upland_total" = upland_total,
    "inundated_total" = inundated_total
  )
  
  return(all_effects)
}

# Run bootstrap with this simplified function
boot_results_interact.robust <- boot(data = df_long_cc, 
                    statistic = boot_psem_with_interactions, 
                    R = 1000)

# Create summary with robust error handling
effect_summary_robust2 <- data.frame(
  Path = names(boot_results_interact.robust$t0),
  Estimate = boot_results_interact.robust$t0,
  CI_Lower = NA_real_,
  CI_Upper = NA_real_,
  Significant = NA,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Calculate CIs with robust error handling
for (i in 1:nrow(effect_summary_robust2)) {
  # Check for NAs in bootstrap samples
  na_count <- sum(is.na(boot_results_interact.robust$t[,i]))
  if (na_count > 0) {
    cat("Parameter", effect_summary_robust2$Path[i], 
        "has", na_count, "missing values out of", nrow(boot_results_interact.robust$t), "\n")
    
    # Skip if too many NAs
    if (na_count > 0.5 * nrow(boot_results_interact.robust$t)) {
      cat("Skipping CI calculation due to excessive missing values\n")
      next
    }
  }
  
  # Try to calculate CI
  tryCatch({
    ci <- boot.ci(boot_results_interact.robust, type = "perc", index = i)
    if (!is.null(ci) && !is.null(ci$percent)) {
      effect_summary_robust2$CI_Lower[i] <- ci$percent[4]
      effect_summary_robust2$CI_Upper[i] <- ci$percent[5]
      effect_summary_robust2$Significant[i] <- (ci$percent[4] * ci$percent[5] > 0)
    }
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("Error calculating CI for parameter", effect_summary_robust2$Path[i], ":", e$message, "\n")
  })
}

# Print summary of valid results
valid_results2 <- effect_summary_robust2[!is.na(effect_summary_robust2$Significant), ]
valid_results2$Estimate <- round(valid_results2$Estimate, 4)
valid_results2$CI_Lower <- round(valid_results2$CI_Lower, 4)
valid_results2$CI_Upper <- round(valid_results2$CI_Upper, 4)

# Sort by effect type
direct_effects <- valid_results2[grep("^direct|^effect", valid_results2$Path), ]
indirect_paths <- valid_results2[grep("via", valid_results2$Path), ]
indirect_totals <- valid_results2[grep("indirect$", valid_results2$Path), ]
total_effects <- valid_results2[grep("total$", valid_results2$Path), ]

# Print results
cat("\n--- DIRECT EFFECTS ---\n")
print(direct_effects[order(-abs(direct_effects$Estimate)), ])

cat("\n--- INDIRECT PATHWAYS ---\n")
print(indirect_paths[order(-abs(indirect_paths$Estimate)), ])

cat("\n--- TOTAL INDIRECT EFFECTS ---\n")
print(indirect_totals)

cat("\n--- TOTAL EFFECTS ---\n")
print(total_effects)

```
#TO DO: ASK claude about these errors *** for robust bootstrap with interactions 


```{r}
# Fit NEE model using different approaches
lm_model <- lm(NEE_umol ~ soil_temp_10_cm + air_temp + VWC + thawdepth + 
               lp_slope + lp_upland + inundated_numeric, data = df_long_cc)

rlm_model <- rlm(NEE_umol ~ soil_temp_10_cm + air_temp + VWC + thawdepth + 
                lp_slope + lp_upland + inundated_numeric, data = df_long_cc)

glm_model <- glm(NEE_umol ~ soil_temp_10_cm + air_temp + VWC + thawdepth + 
                lp_slope + lp_upland + inundated_numeric, 
                family = gaussian(link = "identity"), data = df_long_cc)



# Compare residuals
par(mfrow=c(2,3))
hist(residuals(lm_model), main="LM Residuals")
hist(residuals(rlm_model), main="RLM Residuals")
hist(residuals(glm_model), main="GLM Residuals")


# Compare QQ plots
qqnorm(residuals(lm_model), main="LM QQ Plot"); qqline(residuals(lm_model))
qqnorm(residuals(rlm_model), main="RLM QQ Plot"); qqline(residuals(rlm_model))
qqnorm(residuals(glm_model), main="GLM QQ Plot"); qqline(residuals(glm_model))

#all essentially the same with a strong positive skew, going with robust as it's best able to handle non-normal distributions

```


####using glm 
```{r}
library(boot)
library(piecewiseSEM)

# Simplified bootstrap function focusing just on direct and indirect effects
boot_psem_robust <- function(data, indices) {
  # Create bootstrap sample
  boot_data <- data[indices, ]
  
  # Fit models on bootstrap sample - using robust regression here 
  b_soil <- glm(soil_temp_10_cm ~ lp_slope + lp_upland + inundated_numeric, data = boot_data)
  b_air <- glm(air_temp ~ lp_slope + lp_upland + inundated_numeric, data = boot_data)
  b_vwc <- glm(VWC ~ lp_slope + lp_upland + inundated_numeric, data = boot_data)
  b_thaw <- glm(thawdepth ~ lp_slope + lp_upland + inundated_numeric, data = boot_data)
  b_nee <- glm(NEE_umol ~ soil_temp_10_cm + air_temp + VWC + thawdepth + 
             lp_slope + lp_upland + inundated_numeric, data = boot_data)
  
  # Direct effects
  direct_slope <- coef(b_nee)["lp_slope"]
  direct_upland <- coef(b_nee)["lp_upland"]
  direct_inundated <- coef(b_nee)["inundated_numeric"]
  
  # Environmental effects on NEE
  effect_soil <- coef(b_nee)["soil_temp_10_cm"]
  effect_air <- coef(b_nee)["air_temp"]
  effect_vwc <- coef(b_nee)["VWC"]
  effect_thaw <- coef(b_nee)["thawdepth"]
  
  # Landscape effects on environment
  slope_soil <- coef(b_soil)["lp_slope"]
  slope_air <- coef(b_air)["lp_slope"]
  slope_vwc <- coef(b_vwc)["lp_slope"]
  slope_thaw <- coef(b_thaw)["lp_slope"]
  
  upland_soil <- coef(b_soil)["lp_upland"]
  upland_air <- coef(b_air)["lp_upland"]
  upland_vwc <- coef(b_vwc)["lp_upland"]
  upland_thaw <- coef(b_thaw)["lp_upland"]
  
  inundated_soil <- coef(b_soil)["inundated_numeric"]
  inundated_air <- coef(b_air)["inundated_numeric"]
  inundated_vwc <- coef(b_vwc)["inundated_numeric"]
  inundated_thaw <- coef(b_thaw)["inundated_numeric"]
  
  # Individual indirect paths
  slope_via_soil <- slope_soil * effect_soil
  slope_via_air <- slope_air * effect_air
  slope_via_vwc <- slope_vwc * effect_vwc
  slope_via_thaw <- slope_thaw * effect_thaw
  
  upland_via_soil <- upland_soil * effect_soil
  upland_via_air <- upland_air * effect_air
  upland_via_vwc <- upland_vwc * effect_vwc
  upland_via_thaw <- upland_thaw * effect_thaw
  
  inundated_via_soil <- inundated_soil * effect_soil
  inundated_via_air <- inundated_air * effect_air
  inundated_via_vwc <- inundated_vwc * effect_vwc
  inundated_via_thaw <- inundated_thaw * effect_thaw
  
  # Total indirect effects
  slope_indirect <- slope_via_soil + slope_via_air + slope_via_vwc + slope_via_thaw
  upland_indirect <- upland_via_soil + upland_via_air + upland_via_vwc + upland_via_thaw
  inundated_indirect <- inundated_via_soil + inundated_via_air + inundated_via_vwc + inundated_via_thaw
  
  # Total effects
  slope_total <- direct_slope + slope_indirect
  upland_total <- direct_upland + upland_indirect
  inundated_total <- direct_inundated + inundated_indirect
  
  # Return all values as a named vector
  return(c(
    # Direct effects on NEE
    direct_slope = direct_slope,
    direct_upland = direct_upland,
    direct_inundated = direct_inundated,
    effect_soil = effect_soil,
    effect_air = effect_air,
    effect_vwc = effect_vwc,
    effect_thaw = effect_thaw,
    
    # Indirect paths
    slope_via_soil = slope_via_soil,
    slope_via_air = slope_via_air,
    slope_via_vwc = slope_via_vwc,
    slope_via_thaw = slope_via_thaw,
    upland_via_soil = upland_via_soil,
    upland_via_air = upland_via_air,
    upland_via_vwc = upland_via_vwc,
    upland_via_thaw = upland_via_thaw,
    inundated_via_soil = inundated_via_soil,
    inundated_via_air = inundated_via_air,
    inundated_via_vwc = inundated_via_vwc,
    inundated_via_thaw = inundated_via_thaw,
    
    # Total indirect effects
    slope_indirect = slope_indirect,
    upland_indirect = upland_indirect,
    inundated_indirect = inundated_indirect,
    
    # Total effects
    slope_total = slope_total,
    upland_total = upland_total,
    inundated_total = inundated_total
  ))
}

# Run bootstrap with this simplified function
boot_results_robust <- boot(data = df_long_cc, 
                    statistic = boot_psem_robust, 
                    R = 1000)

# Create summary with robust error handling
effect_summary_robust <- data.frame(
  Path = names(boot_results_robust$t0),
  Estimate = boot_results_robust$t0,
  CI_Lower = NA_real_,
  CI_Upper = NA_real_,
  Significant = NA,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Calculate CIs with robust error handling
for (i in 1:nrow(effect_summary_robust)) {
  # Check for NAs in bootstrap samples
  na_count <- sum(is.na(boot_results_robust$t[,i]))
  if (na_count > 0) {
    cat("Parameter", effect_summary_robust$Path[i], 
        "has", na_count, "missing values out of", nrow(boot_results_robust$t), "\n")
    
    # Skip if too many NAs
    if (na_count > 0.5 * nrow(boot_results_robust$t)) {
      cat("Skipping CI calculation due to excessive missing values\n")
      next
    }
  }
  
  # Try to calculate CI
  tryCatch({
    ci <- boot.ci(boot_results_robust, type = "perc", index = i)
    if (!is.null(ci) && !is.null(ci$percent)) {
      effect_summary_robust$CI_Lower[i] <- ci$percent[4]
      effect_summary_robust$CI_Upper[i] <- ci$percent[5]
      effect_summary_robust$Significant[i] <- (ci$percent[4] * ci$percent[5] > 0)
    }
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("Error calculating CI for parameter", effect_summary_robust$Path[i], ":", e$message, "\n")
  })
}

# Print summary of valid results
valid_results <- effect_summary_robust[!is.na(effect_summary_robust$Significant), ]
valid_resultst$Estimate <- round(valid_results$Estimate, 4)
valid_results$CI_Lower <- round(valid_results$CI_Lower, 4)
valid_results$CI_Upper <- round(valid_results$CI_Upper, 4)

# Sort by effect type
direct_effects <- valid_results[grep("^direct|^effect", valid_results$Path), ]
indirect_paths <- valid_results[grep("via", valid_results$Path), ]
indirect_totals <- valid_results[grep("indirect$", valid_results$Path), ]
total_effects <- valid_results[grep("total$", valid_results$Path), ]

# Print results
cat("\n--- DIRECT EFFECTS ---\n")
print(direct_effects[order(-abs(direct_effects$Estimate)), ])

cat("\n--- INDIRECT PATHWAYS ---\n")
print(indirect_paths[order(-abs(indirect_paths$Estimate)), ])

cat("\n--- TOTAL INDIRECT EFFECTS ---\n")
print(indirect_totals)

cat("\n--- TOTAL EFFECTS ---\n")
print(total_effects)

```
#### p-values from bootstrapping
```{r}
# Calculate p-values from bootstrap distribution
effect_summary$p_value <- NA

for (i in 1:nrow(effect_summary)) {
  if (!is.na(effect_summary$CI_Lower[i])) {
    # Count how many bootstrap samples had opposite sign from estimate
    opposite_sign <- sum((boot_results$t[,i] > 0) != (effect_summary$Estimate[i] > 0), na.rm = TRUE)
    total_samples <- sum(!is.na(boot_results$t[,i]))
    
    # Calculate p-value (two-tailed)
    effect_summary$p_value[i] <- 2 * (opposite_sign / total_samples)
    
    # Cap at 1 (can't have p > 1)
    effect_summary$p_value[i] <- min(effect_summary$p_value[i], 1)
  }
}

# Add significance stars
effect_summary$stars <- ""
effect_summary$stars[effect_summary$p_value < 0.05] <- "*"
effect_summary$stars[effect_summary$p_value < 0.01] <- "**"
effect_summary$stars[effect_summary$p_value < 0.001] <- "***"

# Print updated results with p-values
valid_results <- effect_summary[!is.na(effect_summary$p_value), ]
valid_results$p_value <- round(valid_results$p_value, 4)
print(valid_results[, c("Path", "Estimate", "CI_Lower", "CI_Upper", "p_value", "stars")])
```



#linear relationships 

####RECO vs temp relationship -lm with R2 and p 
```{r}

library(ggplot2)

# Create the plot with points and a linear regression line
soil_RECO <- ggplot(df_RECO, aes(x = soil_temp_10_cm, y = flux_value)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "blue") +
  labs(title = "Relationship between RECO and Soil Temperature",
       x = "Soil Temperature at 10 cm",
       y = "RECO (Carbon Flux)")

# Print the plot
print(soil_RECO)



# Fit a linear model
model <- lm(flux_value ~ soil_temp_10_cm, data = df_RECO, na.action=na.exclude)

# Calculate R² value
r_squared <- summary(model)$r.squared
cat("R² value:", r_squared, "\n")

# Test for significance
summary(model)


#Add R2 to the figure 
# Fit a linear model
model <- lm(flux_value ~ soil_temp_10_cm, data = df_RECO)

# Calculate R² value
r_squared <- summary(model)$r.squared

# Create the plot with points, a linear regression line, and R² annotation
soil_RECO2 <- ggplot(df_RECO, aes(x = soil_temp_10_cm, y = flux_value)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "blue") +
  annotate("text", x = Inf, y = Inf, label = paste("R² =", round(r_squared, 2)),
           hjust = 1.1, vjust = 1.1, size = 5, color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Relationship between RECO and Soil Temperature",
       x = "Soil Temperature at 10 cm",
       y = "RECO (Carbon Flux)")+
  theme_minimal()

# Print the plot
print(soil_RECO2)

```


#TO DO: how enviro variables change by landpos, and from year to year?
